findings.message: The precedent citation is wrong: the field-edge-renderer discussion in ADR `2026-04-26-0855` is in `S2`, not `D2`. `D2` is the `expected_members` decision, so this reference sends readers to the wrong design rationale.

This rule is internally inconsistent: the renderer lookup is described here as limited to `secondary_port`-indexed entries, but the decision later requires the same path to cover `application_service` too. That cannot work as written, and it also conflicts with the kind-selection convention where `application_service` is usecase-only and `secondary_adapter` is infrastructure-only.

This references `R7 (Cross-Track Port Reference)` as if it already exists in `knowledge/conventions/type-designer-kind-selection.md`, but that convention currently only defines R1-R6. The ADR should frame this as a new rule to be added, not an existing SSoT entry.

The context treats `application_service` as a valid declaration target for a `secondary_adapter` implementation, but the kind-selection convention makes `application_service` usecase-only and `secondary_adapter` infrastructure-only. That failure case should be limited to `secondary_port`.

AC-05 is assigned only to T001, but T001 is explicitly documentation-only ('Rust コード変更なし') and does not touch any type catalogue or the contract-map renderer. As written, the plan has no task that can produce the required `secondary_port` registration / `-.impl.->` edge behavior, so this acceptance criterion is not physically realizable from the scheduled work.

AC-05 is enforced as completed by T001/T002/T003, but it requires a `reference` secondary_port to be registered and a concrete `FsReviewStore -.impl.-> ReviewReader`-style edge gap to be resolved. The same spec explicitly puts retrofitting past-track catalogues out of scope (OS-06), and the planned tasks are only convention docs, typestate-edge rendering, and tests. As written, this acceptance criterion is not executable within the stated scope.

The S2 section describes generating a Mermaid edge as `==>=|transitions_to|`, which contradicts the spec/T002 requirement to use the `==>` edge with a `transitions_to` label (`==>|"transitions_to"|`). This malformed notation can send implementers toward the wrong renderer output; fix the impl-plan source and regenerate `plan.md`.

AC-05 requires a unit test (or new test catalogue) proving an `action: "reference"` secondary_port is indexed and produces a `-.impl.->` edge from a secondary_adapter, but the plan's only test task adds five typestate transition tests and never exercises reference-port impl-edge behavior. The plan can therefore complete T001-T003 while leaving AC-05 unsatisfied, despite the coverage artifact assigning AC-05 to those tasks.

`AC-05` is covered here by `T001`, `T002`, and `T003`, but the plan text only assigns the reference-port impl-edge verification to `T003`; `T001` is convention-only and `T002` is limited to typestate transition rendering. This makes the coverage artifact semantically disagree with the implementation plan about which tasks actually satisfy `AC-05`, so progress/gate readers can believe the acceptance criterion is enforced by tasks that never exercise it.

CN-04 references `CN-08 ノイズ抑制`, but this plan artifact defines only CN-01 through CN-07 and does not provide any file/anchor for CN-08. The generated `spec.md` mirrors the same unresolved reference, so readers cannot resolve the constraint being invoked. Either remove the parenthetical or replace it with a resolvable reference.

GO-02 says `methods_of()` will read `TypestateTransitions::To(names)` and generate transition edges, but the implementation plan later directs T002 to add a separate `render_contract_map` edge-loop block that emits `==>|"transitions_to"|` edges. Those are different design loci, and `methods_of()` is otherwise described as feeding method-call edges, so this top-level goal can send implementers toward the wrong mechanism. Align GO-02/spec.md with the T002 edge-generation path.
